SEARCH
Package com.group02.tars.controller.api

Class BaseApiServlet

java.lang.Object
jakarta.servlet.GenericServlet
jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet
com.group02.tars.controller.api.BaseApiServlet
All Implemented Interfaces:
jakarta.servlet.Servlet, jakarta.servlet.ServletConfig, Serializable
Direct Known Subclasses:
AdminApiServlet, AiApiServlet, AuthApiServlet, CvFileServlet, MoApiServlet, TaApiServlet
public abstract class BaseApiServlet extends jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet
Base servlet for shared API concerns such as service lookup, session checks, JSON request parsing, and JSON error responses.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

    registry
    Shared service registry initialized for API servlet subclasses.

    Writes a service-layer error as the standard JSON error envelope.

    writeUnknownError(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse resp, Exception ex)
    Writes an unexpected exception as a standard internal error response.

  • Method Details

    • init

      public void init() throws jakarta.servlet.ServletException
      Initializes the shared service registry for API subclasses.
      Overrides:
      init in class jakarta.servlet.GenericServlet
      Throws:
      jakarta.servlet.ServletException - if the service registry cannot be initialized

    • notImplemented

      protected void notImplemented(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse resp, String scope) throws IOException
      Writes a standard not-implemented error for endpoint groups not yet available.
      Parameters:
      req - current request
      resp - current response
      scope - endpoint group label
      Throws:
      IOException - if the JSON response cannot be written

    • readBodyAsMap

      protected Map<String,Object> readBodyAsMap(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req) throws IOException, ServiceException
      Reads the request body as a JSON object.
      Parameters:
      req - current request
      Returns:
      parsed JSON body, or an empty map when there is no body
      Throws:
      IOException - if the request stream cannot be read
      ServiceException - if the body is not a valid JSON object

    • asString

      protected String asString(Map<String,Object> body, String key)
      Reads a trimmed string value from a parsed request body.
      Parameters:
      body - parsed request body
      key - map key to read
      Returns:
      trimmed string value, or an empty string when absent

    • queryInt

      protected int queryInt(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req, String key, int defaultValue)
      Parses an integer query parameter with a fallback value.
      Parameters:
      req - current request
      key - query parameter name
      defaultValue - value returned when the parameter is blank or invalid
      Returns:
      parsed integer or the default value

    • requireSessionUser

      protected User requireSessionUser(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse resp, String... allowedRoles) throws IOException
      Validates the current session and optional role requirements.
      Parameters:
      req - current request
      resp - current response
      allowedRoles - roles allowed for the endpoint; empty means any logged-in role
      Returns:
      session user without password, or null after writing an error response
      Throws:
      IOException - if the JSON error response cannot be written

    • writeServiceError

      protected void writeServiceError(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse resp, ServiceException ex) throws IOException
      Writes a service-layer error as the standard JSON error envelope.
      Parameters:
      req - current request
      resp - current response
      ex - service exception to serialize
      Throws:
      IOException - if the JSON response cannot be written

    • writeUnknownError

      protected void writeUnknownError(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse resp, Exception ex) throws IOException
      Writes an unexpected exception as a standard internal error response.
      Parameters:
      req - current request
      resp - current response
      ex - exception to report
      Throws:
      IOException - if the JSON response cannot be written
